final_text:
The answer is clear. `FileField.clean` in `django/forms/fields.py` is the method that handles all three states (new file, clear, keep initial), raises `FILE_INPUT_CONTRADICTION`, and delegates to `super().clean()` (i.e., `Field.clean` → `to_python`) only when there's actual new data.

```json
{"file":"django/forms/fields.py","class":"FileField","method":"clean","parent_class":"Field","fix_summary":"FileField.clean() distinguishes all three states — raising a ValidationError for FILE_INPUT_CONTRADICTION when the widget sees both a new upload and a clear checkbox, returning False (or the initial value) when the user clears or leaves the field unchanged, and only delegating to to_python() via super().clean() when the user has actually submitted a new file."}
```

parsed_answer.fix_summary: FileField.clean() distinguishes all three states — raising a ValidationError for FILE_INPUT_CONTRADICTION when the widget sees both a new upload and a clear checkbox, returning False (or the initial value) when the user clears or leaves the field unchanged, and only delegating to to_python() via super().clean() when the user has actually submitted a new file.
